texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-29 one more bounded exact-host New Hampshire DHHS pass. `https://www.dhhs.nh.gov/`, `https://dhhs.nh.gov/`, and `https://www.nh.gov/dhhs/` all still return the same public HTTP 403 `Access Denied` shell rather than any reviewable public DHHS content. The robots lanes are publicly reachable again, but still do not reopen a reviewable proof lane: `https://www.nh.gov/robots.txt` and `https://www.dhhs.nh.gov/robots.txt` now return HTTP 200 robots text that only confirms host existence and crawl rules. New Hampshire therefore still lacks a reviewable public DHHS lane for the blocked health, DD, EI, waiver, and county-local families.

Reviewed 2026-06-29 one more bounded exact-host New Hampshire education pass. `https://education.nh.gov/`, `https://www.education.nh.gov/`, `https://www.nh.gov/education/`, and `https://my.doe.nh.gov/ehb/` all still return the same public HTTP 403 `Access Denied` shell rather than any reviewable statewide or local education-routing content. `https://education.nh.gov/robots.txt` is publicly reachable again, but it only returns HTTP 200 robots text and does not reopen a district-grade routing contract. The separate official federal IDEA-by-State page at `https://sites.ed.gov/idea/state/new-hampshire/` remains live and keeps statewide IDEA Part B authority verified, but it still does not provide district-, county-, or SAU-grade routing. New Hampshire therefore still lacks a reviewable public DOE lane for local education routing.

The robots lanes are publicly reachable again, but still do not reopen a reviewable proof lane: `https://www.nh.gov/robots.txt` and `https://www.dhhs.nh.gov/robots.txt` now return HTTP 200 robots text that only confirms host existence and crawl rules. New Hampshire therefore still lacks a reviewable public DHHS lane for the blocked health, DD, EI, waiver, and county-local families.

Reviewed 2026-06-29 one more bounded exact-host New Hampshire employment-security / VR pass. `https://nhes.nh.gov/`, `https://www.nhes.nh.gov/`, and `https://www.nh.gov/nhes/` all still return the same public HTTP 403 `Access Denied` shell rather than any reviewable VR or Pre-ETS content. The robots lane is publicly reachable again, but it only returns HTTP 200 robots text and does not restore a public vocational-rehabilitation or Pre-ETS contract. New Hampshire therefore still lacks a reviewable public VR successor lane.
